claim: T. Savo (handle Kevlar) is the architect of the assembly described by the umbrella manifest at provenance/v1/umbrella.json. The manifest enumerates 12 content-addressed files (manifesto sections §11 and §12, the architectural derivation `the-pieces-on-the-table.md`, the path-to-default doc, and the seven 2026-05-* specs plus the v1.4.1 protocol catalog and the Locus addendum to ir-formal-grammar.md). The architectural moves named in those documents — the multi-dimensional address (§11), the rank-N pin (§12), the contract-vs-attestation CID separation (#91), the contractSetCid as the content-only set vector (#94), the envelope/header/body layering (#95), the three-axis pinning convention (#96), the bridge target-dimensionality with R6 anchoring kits at the source-of-truth contractCid (#97), the derived bridge linkage protocol where the linker mints bridges from the union of contracts and call edges (#114), the linker-daemon-protocol that makes LSP+linker tractable at typing speed (#126), and the normative Locus addendum that pins the JCS shape of source positions (#120) — are the architect's contribution. The pieces beneath them (BLAKE3-512, JCS, Ed25519, LSP, content-addressing as a primitive) are public; the assembly into a cross-language compile-time correctness substrate is the named contribution.
declaredAt: <UTC ISO-8601 timestamp at signing time, e.g. 2026-05-03T18:30:00Z>
signature: <ed25519:base64-signature over JCS({kind, version, umbrellaCid, claim, declaredAt, signer})>
_signing_instructions: After filling signer + declaredAt fields above, JCS-encode the object with the `_signing_instructions` and `signature` fields removed, sign the resulting bytes with your Ed25519 private key, and put the base64 signature in the signature field. The canonicalizer in tools/foundation-keygen/ has the JCS routine; alternatively use any JCS implementation that conforms to RFC 8785.
